title: Warm Greek Feta Tiropita
description: A satvik-friendly, herbal, and pillowy Greek dish made with speck and wood-fired baked. Perfect for meal prep and an easy weeknight dinner.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400°F (200°C) with a wood-fired baking stone inside, if available.
Thaw the phyllo dough according to package instructions.
In a large mixing bowl, crumble the feta cheese and add chopped speck, salt, and black pepper. Mix well.
Brush the bottom phyllo sheet with olive oil and place it on a flat surface.
Place one-sixth of the feta-speck mixture onto the center of the phyllo sheet, leaving a 2-inch border around it.
Fold the 2-inch border over the filling, then roll the phyllo into a tight log. Place seam-side down on a baking sheet.
Repeat with the remaining phyllo sheets and filling mixture.
Place the tiropita logs on the preheated baking stone and bake for 15-20 minutes, or until golden brown.
Remove from the oven and let cool for 5 minutes before slicing.
Garnish with chopped fresh dill, lemon zest, and parsley before serving.
